name: Athena RPC: getMessage (cereal message proxy)
description: Add the getMessage openpilot athenad RPC method. Takes a cereal service name (e.g., 'carState', 'deviceState', 'liveLocationKalman') and an optional timeout in ms, and returns the next message from that service as a JSON dict. Reference: openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py lines 340-357 (../../openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py). This is the primary mechanism for reading live device telemetry over the control channel; device-status-panel will depend on it.
acceptance_criteria: internal/ws/methods.go defines GetMessageParams { Service string `json:"service"`; Timeout int `json:"timeout,omitempty"` }
CallGetMessage(caller, client, service string, timeoutMs int) (map[string]interface{}, error) is added; if timeoutMs <= 0, default to 1000 to match athenad's default
The RPC call itself uses CallWithTimeout so the Go-side context cancels a few hundred ms after the device-side timeout (add a small buffer)
RegisterDefaultHandlers installs a device-side stub that returns a canned message dict keyed by the service name
internal/ws/methods_test.go covers the happy path, invalid-service error, and the timeout-buffer behavior
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
